ev.headline: BYD and CATL Begin Small-Scale Solid-State Battery Production in 2027
ev.summary: Chinese battery giants BYD and CATL, which account for over 55% of global EV battery sales, plan to begin small-scale production of solid-state batteries in 2027. This advanced technology aims to enable 800+ mile driving ranges.

ev.headline: Toyota Invests $1 Billion in US EV Manufacturing
ev.summary: Toyota announced a $1 billion investment across Kentucky and Indiana facilities, with $800 million allocated to Georgetown, Kentucky for manufacturing a second domestic EV alongside the electric Highlander.

ev.headline: Sion Power Pivots to Defense Industry for Lithium-Metal Batteries
ev.summary: EV battery startup Sion Power is shifting focus from electric vehicles to aerospace and defense applications, developing lithium-metal batteries for military drones due to weak EV market demand and geopolitical opportunities.

ev.headline: Mercedes-Benz Shifts Strategy to Hybrid Focus Amid EV Demand Weakness
ev.summary: Mercedes-Benz is emphasizing mild-hybrid technology in its volume segments, offering new GLB models at lower prices to counter weak luxury EV sales and tackle inventory challenges in the US market.

ev.headline: China's EV Sales Drop 26% YTD Due to Weakened Fiscal Support
ev.summary: China experienced its lowest monthly EV sales since February 2024 as reduced government subsidies dampened demand. Sales are down 26% in the first two months of 2026 compared to 2025.

ev.headline: EU EV Market Share Reaches 18.8% in Early 2026
ev.summary: Battery-electric vehicles captured 18.8% of the EU market share in January-February 2026, up from 15.2% a year earlier, while hybrids remain the preferred choice at 38.7% market share.

ev.headline: Chery Unveils Solid-State Batteries with 1,500 km Range
ev.summary: Chinese automaker Chery announced new solid-state batteries achieving 600 Wh/kg energy density and 1,500 km CLTC driving range, with vehicle testing planned for next year starting with the Exeed ES8.

ev.headline: BYD Outsells Tesla in Europe for Second Consecutive Month
ev.summary: BYD sold 17,954 vehicles in February 2026 compared to Tesla's 17,664, marking the second consecutive month BYD leads in European EV sales with an expanding year-to-date advantage.

ev.headline: US EV Sales Down 26.8% Year-Over-Year in February
ev.summary: US new EV sales totaled 68,951 units in February 2026, down 26.8% year-over-year but up 5.8% month-over-month, with EVs accounting for 5.8% of total new vehicle sales as market adjusts post-tax credit.

ev.headline: 18 Major Automakers Cancel or Delay EV Plans Amid Market Slowdown
ev.summary: At least 18 automakers including Ford, Honda, Nissan, and Volkswagen are now canceling, delaying, or scaling back EV plans in the US following falling demand and expiring federal tax credits.

ev.headline: Rivian R2 Launching Spring 2026 with Starting Price Under $60,000
ev.summary: Rivian's highly anticipated R2 will launch this spring for just under $60,000, with the Performance version offering 330 miles range, dual motors, and 656 hp, positioning it as a key affordable EV option.

ev.headline: NEVI Charging Program Simplified with New Trump Administration Guidance
ev.summary: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy unveiled revised NEVI guidance to streamline EV charging infrastructure deployment, eliminating complex requirements and giving states more flexibility to build chargers efficiently.

ev.headline: Detroit's $114 Billion EV Investment Loss Signals Strategic Miscalculation
ev.summary: Seven major automakers have lost over $114 billion on EV investments due to slower-than-expected demand and expired incentives, representing one of the most dramatic strategic miscalculations in automotive history.

ev.headline: Stellantis Records $26.3 Billion Loss from EV Strategy Reset
ev.summary: Stellantis posted a staggering $26.3 billion net loss for 2025 with $30 billion in write-downs largely tied to scaling back its aggressive EV strategy after consumer demand failed to materialize as expected.

ev.headline: Sodium-Ion Battery Breakthrough: BAIC Achieves 4C Fast Charging
ev.summary: Beijing Automotive Group completed its first sodium-ion battery prototype with energy density over 170Wh/kg and achieved 4C fast charging in 11 minutes, nearing mass production viability.

energy.headline: Britain Requires Solar Panels and Heat Pumps in All New Homes as Iran War Reshapes Energy Policy
energy.summary: The U.K. government introduced new rules requiring developers to install heat pumps and solar panels in all new homes across England starting in 2028 in response to energy security concerns from regional conflict.

energy.headline: Utilities Push Back Against Plug-In Solar Expansion, Delaying Bills in Multiple States
energy.summary: Electric utilities are raising safety concerns about plug-in solar panels, prompting delays in legislation across Georgia, Arizona, New Mexico, Washington and Wyoming despite over 30 states considering similar bills.

energy.headline: California Court Upholds Low Net Metering Rates, Setback for Rooftop Solar Advocates
energy.summary: A California appeals court sided with state regulators in reducing the rates utilities pay customers for excess solar energy, disappointing environmental groups seeking to maximize solar adoption.

energy.headline: Illinois Advances Plug-In Solar Legislation as 30+ States Embrace DIY Solar Systems
energy.summary: More than two dozen states are advancing bills to legalize and simplify plug-in solar installation, with Illinois now pushing forward legislation as homeowners increasingly seek affordable alternatives to expensive rooftop systems.

energy.headline: Batteries Become the New Home Solar as Net Metering Changes Drive Energy Independence Strategy
energy.summary: As net metering policies reduce compensation for grid exports, homeowners increasingly prioritize battery storage over selling solar power back to utilities, shifting the home energy market toward self-consumption models.

energy.headline: US Residential Solar Enters Post-Incentive Era After Historic 205% Tax Credit Surge
energy.summary: The elimination of the 30% federal tax credit for purchased residential solar systems triggered a 205% surge in installations in late 2025, creating supply constraints that reshaped the entire residential energy market.

energy.headline: 86 GW of Capacity Planned in 2026 as Solar and Storage Dominate U.S. Energy Expansion
energy.summary: Project developers are preparing for a historic grid expansion with 86 GW of new utility-scale capacity planned for 2026, with solar and battery storage accounting for 79% of all planned additions.

energy.headline: Third-Party Solar Ownership Models Reshape US Market After Federal Tax Credit Expiration
energy.summary: As federal tax credits for purchased residential solar systems expire, third-party ownership options like leases and power purchase agreements are becoming the most popular financing model among residential installers.

energy.headline: Connecticut Increases Net Metering Charges, Raising Costs for New Solar Customers in 2026
energy.summary: Connecticut increased non-bypassable charges on solar energy from 0.5¢ to 3.25¢ per kilowatt-hour for new residential customers starting January 1, 2026, affecting the economics of new solar installations.

energy.headline: Home Electrification Market Shifts to Integrated Energy Systems Beyond Solar Alone
energy.summary: Homeowners are increasingly planning coordinated home energy systems combining solar, storage, EV charging, and heat pumps rather than shopping for individual products, reshaping installer business models.

energy.headline: Solar and Storage to Lead Record 86 GW Grid Expansion in 2026
energy.summary: Solar and battery storage will account for 79% of the 86 GW of new utility-scale capacity projected to come online in 2026, with solar generation expected to grow from 290 TWh to over 420 TWh by year-end.

energy.headline: Colorado Advances 'Meter Collar' Technology to Enable Affordable Home Solar Without Panel Upgrades
energy.summary: Colorado legislators are advancing a bill to legalize 'meter collars' that allow full rooftop solar arrays with battery storage at a cost of $500-$700, avoiding expensive $10,000 electrical panel upgrades.

energy.headline: Battery Attachment Rates Hit 69% in California as Net Billing Drives Storage Growth
energy.summary: California's residential battery attachment rate reached 69% as the state's Net Billing policy reduces the value of exporting solar power, incentivizing homeowners to pair solar with storage for self-consumption.

energy.headline: Energy Storage Manufacturing Surge Doubles Projections as EV Battery Makers Pivot to Stationary Storage
energy.summary: Downturns in the electric vehicle market are prompting battery manufacturers to shift production toward stationary energy storage, doubling industry projections for total battery capacity by 2030.

energy.headline: New York Offers $3,000 Battery Storage Incentives as State Accelerates Home Energy Transition
energy.summary: New York homeowners installing battery storage systems from FranklinWH can receive $3,000 in upfront incentives and earn approximately $200 per year through grid participation programs with National Grid and Orange & Rockland.

energy.headline: Stanford Study Finds 60% of U.S. Households Can Save Money with Solar-Plus-Storage Systems
energy.summary: A Stanford University analysis found that about 60% of American families could reduce electricity costs by 15% on average by installing solar-battery systems, accounting for equipment capital and operating costs.

energy.headline: U.S. Battery Energy Storage Capacity Reaches 166.1 GWh, Up 53% Year-Over-Year
energy.summary: U.S. battery energy storage capacity surged to 166.1 GWh, up 53% from the end of 2023, enough to power every American home for 58 minutes or 5 million homes for an entire year.

energy.headline: Plug-In Solar Systems Growing 80% Annually as Battery Prices Plummet
energy.summary: Residential battery storage installations are accelerating at record growth rates as lithium-ion battery costs continue falling, with new markets opening up for affordable, easy-to-install plug-in solar systems.

energy.headline: California Battery Attachment Rate Reaches 69% Amid Net Billing Transition
energy.summary: California's residential solar battery attachment rate climbed to 69% as homeowners respond to the state's Net Billing Tariff policy by pairing storage with solar to maximize self-consumption.

energy.headline: Global Solar-Plus-Storage Market Projected to Reach $59.8 Billion by 2034
energy.summary: The global solar energy storage battery market is projected to grow from $7.84 billion in 2026 to $59.82 billion by 2034, exhibiting a compound annual growth rate of 28.93%.

energy.headline: Solar Energy Industries Association Reports Record 58 GWh of Energy Storage Installed in 2025
energy.summary: The U.S. installed a record 58 GWh of new energy storage capacity in 2025, four times what the industry deployed just three years ago, driven by declining battery costs and grid reliability needs.

energy.headline: North Carolina Offers $9,000 in Combined Solar and Battery Rebates Through Duke Energy Program
energy.summary: North Carolina homeowners can receive up to $9,000 in combined rebates through Duke Energy's PowerPair program for installing both solar systems and battery storage, with property tax exemptions also available.

energy.headline: Solar Retrofit Market Emerging as Growth Opportunity for Battery Storage on Existing Systems
energy.summary: A large installed base of solar-only homes created between 2016 and 2023 represents a significant untapped market opportunity for battery retrofits, as net metering policies shift and outage risks increase.

energy.headline: California Residential Solar Battery Attachment Rates Exceed 30% Following Net Metering Reforms
energy.summary: California's residential solar battery attachment rates surpassed 30% in 2023 following net metering reforms, with over 40,000 new solar-plus-storage installations added between October 2023 and April 2024.

v2x.headline: Tesla Cybertruck Gets Vehicle-to-Grid Capabilities with Powershare Grid Support
v2x.summary: Tesla launches Powershare Grid Support program in Texas, enabling Cybertruck to function as a distributed power plant and earn owners bill credits.

v2x.headline: Polestar and dcbel Launch Vehicle-to-Home for Polestar 3 in California
v2x.summary: Polestar partners with dcbel to roll out V2H, blackout backup, and smart charging for Polestar 3 owners starting in California.

v2x.headline: Top 10 EV Battery Trends in 2025 and What We Can Expect in 2026
v2x.summary: CALSTART identifies enhanced grid integration as key trend, showing EV batteries can send energy back to grid during peak demand.

v2x.headline: Xos Announces V2G Production Beginning April 2026 for School Bus Platform
v2x.summary: Commercial EV builder Xos launches V2G capability on electric school buses, creating direct revenue streams for school districts without hardware retrofits.

v2x.headline: Retired EV Batteries Bolstering Texas Power Grid
v2x.summary: B2U Storage Solutions and Element Energy deploy used EV batteries for grid storage, providing lower-cost energy storage and grid support.

v2x.headline: Inside Climate News: Retired EV Batteries Score New Gig Bolstering Texas Grid
v2x.summary: Used GM EV batteries deployed in Bexar County provide grid stability and revenue streams, with potential for 15-year second-life usage.

v2x.headline: Nissan to Launch Affordable Vehicle-to-Grid Technology in 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan announces affordable bidirectional charging with AC-based solutions targeting UK and European markets, enabling lower-cost V2G access.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for V2H and V2G
v2x.summary: Enphase announces new DC bidirectional charger architecture enabling vehicle-to-home and vehicle-to-grid capabilities, expected H2 2026.

v2x.headline: Electric Cars Can Make Power Grids More Reliable (and Earn Owners Money)
v2x.summary: NC State study examines V2G adoption barriers in the US, identifying standardization and infrastructure readiness as key challenges.

v2x.headline: 2026 Outlook on V2G Support for Energy Management
v2x.summary: Nuvve reports V2G has moved from pilot phase to backbone of battery aggregation with fleet operators and utilities as primary adopters.

v2x.headline: GM Energy Leads Vehicle-to-Home EV Segment
v2x.summary: General Motors dominates V2H market with 246,000 EVs equipped with technology across Ultium platform, offering comprehensive energy ecosystem.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid Market Surges to USD 11.25 Billion by 2033
v2x.summary: V2G market projected to reach $11.25 billion by 2033 with 18.5% CAGR, driven by falling charger costs and standardized protocols.

v2x.headline: The Battery on Wheels Revolution: $5.8 Billion Vehicle-to-Grid Era Begins
v2x.summary: Global bidirectional EV charging market projected to reach $5.8 billion by 2036, with 2026 identified as the critical takeoff point.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid Conference 2026 in Münster
v2x.summary: International V2G, V2H & Smart Charging conference April 15-16, 2026 brings together automotive, energy, and technology leaders.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Chargers Review - V2G & V2H
v2x.summary: Comprehensive review of bidirectional chargers including Wallbox Quasar 2, Sigenergy SigenStor, and Ambibox systems for V2G/V2H applications.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging in 2026: Which Cars Support It?
v2x.summary: Comprehensive guide showing Ford F-150 Lightning, Tesla Cybertruck, GM Ultium vehicles, and others now supporting V2H and V2G capabilities.

v2x.headline: Grid Integration of EV Batteries Benefits Drivers and Energy Providers
v2x.summary: Center for Sustainable Energy discusses how V2G and V2H enable energy independence, backup power, and grid stability while supporting virtual power plants.

v2x.headline: BMW and E.ON Launch V2G Offering with BMW iX3
v2x.summary: BMW presents first commercial V2G offering for Germany with E.ON, using BMW iX3 and bidirectional BMW Wallbox Professional.

v2x.headline: Installing Bidirectional Charging Solutions: Overcoming Complexities
v2x.summary: Qmerit discusses technical and installation complexity of bidirectional charging, certified electrician requirements, and emerging automaker support.

v2x.headline: What Is Bidirectional Charging? Understanding Benefits for Drivers and Businesses
v2x.summary: EV Connect explains bidirectional charging technology, benefits for cost savings and grid stability, and available chargers like Wallbox and Nuvve.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging and Electric Vehicles for Mobile Storage
v2x.summary: Department of Energy details V2B and V2G solutions for mobile battery storage, demand response, and grid resilience integration.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging: Future Trends & Use Cases
v2x.summary: Driivz analyzes bidirectional charging trends, VPP integration, and potential for EV batteries to save 22 billion euros annually by 2040 in Europe.

v2x.headline: What's Next for EV Batteries in 2026
v2x.summary: MIT Technology Review explores EV battery developments including sodium-ion alternatives, geopolitical shifts, and emerging battery chemistries.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Charging: Everything You Need to Know
v2x.summary: Comprehensive guide to V2L, V2H, V2G capabilities, cost savings potential, vehicle compatibility, and future adoption trends.
